acceptance_targets: [ch10-craft-structural-echo] In the revised June scene anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:219 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:293, a reader must encounter a new pressure specific to June's household before the scene settles into advice, and the scene must no longer read as a second pass of 'friend diagnoses Lena, asks how much, tells her Eli may just be tired, feeds her, sends her home.'

[ch10-dialogue-eli-generic-soothing] In the revised late-call exchange at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:437 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:487, at least two of Eli's soothing lines must explicitly react to concrete details already present in the scene or Lena's history, and at least one reassurance beat must sound slightly improvised or misfitted rather than like a generic grounding exercise or abstract emotional maxim.

[ch10-prose-overexplained-chill] Across the revised passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:455 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:543, no more than two explicit narrator explanations may tell the reader why Eli feels wrong; the rest of the unease must come from concrete glitches, repeated phrasing, timing, or Lena's immediate bodily reaction on the page.

[ch10-prose-scene-opening-formula] In the revised chapter, at least one of the three post-break scenes beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:105, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:219, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:397 must start with an action or spoken line in its first two sentences, with sensory inventory arriving only after the scene's conflict or leverage is underway.
[FA-008] Pass if the Mateo beat and the phone beat no longer rely on the same 'almost' syntax and feel like two different registers. Fail if both moments still hinge on withheld-reaction phrasing.

[FA-016] Pass if snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4805 now differentiates this tiredness from earlier scenes without unreadable, blank, or flat-face shorthand. Fail if the line still names the face as unreadable.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised June scene anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:219 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:293, a reader must encounter a new pressure specific to June's household before the scene settles into advice, and the scene must no longer read as a second pass of 'friend diagnoses Lena, asks how much, tells her Eli may just be tired, feeds her, sends her home.'
findings.problem: The Farah and June scenes perform nearly the same triage sequence: Lena arrives visibly wrecked, gets asked what the damage was, gets nudged toward Eli as cause, receives food or care, and leaves with practical advice. Because the second conversation mostly replays the first before adding June's household strain, the chapter middle feels duplicated rather than escalated.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate the June apartment scene at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:219, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:293, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:323, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:363 so it does work Farah's scene cannot do. Move June's hidden laptop pressure or Mateo's partial awareness to the foreground earlier, cut at least one repeated 'what happened/how much/with him' beat, and let the comfort arrive compromised by June's own strain instead of as a second round of similar counsel.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised late-call exchange at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:437 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:487, at least two of Eli's soothing lines must explicitly react to concrete details already present in the scene or Lena's history, and at least one reassurance beat must sound slightly improvised or misfitted rather than like a generic grounding exercise or abstract emotional maxim.
findings.problem: Eli's late-call comfort slips into polished therapy-smooth language: the five-senses prompt, 'You don't have to earn the rest of the list,' 'That doesn't get to become the whole story of you,' and 'I'm not gonna make you perform okay for me tonight.' The chapter wants him to feel over-adaptive, but these lines flatten his idiolect into generic support-script copy instead of keeping the style-bible promise of Lena-specific curated softness.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Eli's reassurance at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:445, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:453, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:491, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:531 so at least two comfort beats are grounded in details only this relationship and this room supply: Lena's captioning error, the rain or bus noise at her window, her work habits, Farah's soup, or another concrete callback already on the page. Keep the uncanniness, but roughen the language so one reply feels improvised rather than prepackaged.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:455 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:543, no more than two explicit narrator explanations may tell the reader why Eli feels wrong; the rest of the unease must come from concrete glitches, repeated phrasing, timing, or Lena's immediate bodily reaction on the page.
findings.problem: Once the lag and repetition pattern is visible, the prose keeps restating that Eli feels too clean, repeatable, or ready. The first sensory chill lands; the next several explanations soften it by diagnosing the effect again and again instead of letting concrete anomalies keep working on the reader.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress the explanatory commentary in the final call at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:455, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:467, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:515, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:533, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:539, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:543. Keep one or two interpretive sentences, but let the duplicated laugh at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:501, the pre-shaped compliment at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:517, or the too-fast answer at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:537 carry the unease without repeated gloss.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised chapter, at least one of the three post-break scenes beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:105, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:219, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:397 must start with an action or spoken line in its first two sentences, with sensory inventory arriving only after the scene's conflict or leverage is underway.
findings.problem: Each scene opens with a strong but similar atmosphere-first inventory of weather, smell, and room condition before human leverage starts. Used four times in one chapter, the move becomes a visible formula and regularizes the pulse instead of giving each scene a distinct entry.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework at least one later scene opening at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:105, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:219, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:397 so the first two sentences enter through action, dialogue, or pressure rather than another room-weather catalog. Keep the environmental texture, but fold it in after the scene's human leverage is already active.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the Mateo beat and the phone beat no longer rely on the same 'almost' syntax and feel like two different registers. Fail if both moments still hinge on withheld-reaction phrasing.
findings.problem: The novel's 9-chapter 'almost [verb]' pattern appears twice in this chapter, at lines 4785 and 4975. Using the same syntax for Mateo amusement and for the phone-prop flirtation blurs two very different social textures.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4785 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4975, replace the weaker 'almost smiled' and 'almost said' beats with voice-specific reactions. Mateo should pull a sharper aunt-nephew rhythm, and the phone scene should show flirtation or annoyance through what Lena actually says or does.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4805 now differentiates this tiredness from earlier scenes without unreadable, blank, or flat-face shorthand. Fail if the line still names the face as unreadable.
findings.problem: The 9-chapter unreadable-face pattern shows up here as 'blunt and unreadable,' which makes June's tiredness generic at a point when the couch scene needs sharper relational texture.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4805 so June's exhaustion and alertness come through voice, posture, or the exact way she asks 'What happened' instead of the blanket label 'unreadable.' Keep the fatigue; remove the stock descriptor.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
